skill.name: quapas-quality-assessment-for-prognosis-studies
skill.description: Evaluates bias in medical literature (prognosis studies) using QUAPAS criteria. Use when the user wants to assess the quality or risk of bias of a medical paper text.
skill.summary: Standardized bias assessment for prognosis studies using the validated QUAPAS framework across 5 domains.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 53 static findings evaluated. 52 are false positives from pattern matching errors (Markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution, QUAPAS acronym misidentified as cryptographic algorithm, medical screening questions misidentified as reconnaissance). 1 true positive (file write in extract_pdf.py:53) is benign - it writes extracted PDF text to an output file, which is the script's documented purpose. The skill's own evaluation shows all 20/20 assertions passing with a Production Ready grade.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown backticks flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly identified Markdown inline code formatting (single backticks around file paths like scripts/extract_pdf.py) and fenced code blocks as Ruby/shell backtick command execution. These are documentation examples showing users how to run the helper script, not active shell execution commands. No actual command injection risk exists.

low_findings.title: False positive: QUAPAS acronym flagged as cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the medical research acronym QUAPAS (Quality of Prognosis Studies) as a weak cryptographic algorithm across 26 locations. QUAPAS is a validated medical bias assessment tool, not a cryptographic algorithm. No cryptographic operations exist in this skill.

low_findings.title: False positive: Standard QUAPAS questions flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged standard medical screening questions (e.g., Was a case-control design avoided? Did all participants receive the index test?) from the QUAPAS framework as system reconnaissance. These are validated domain-specific questions for prognosis study quality assessment, not attempts to gather system information.

low_findings.title: False positive: GitHub source URL flagged as hardcoded network URL
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills as a hardcoded URL. This is a legitimate attribution link to the open-source repository for this skill. No network exfiltration or unauthorized data transmission risk exists.

low_findings.title: Benign file write in PDF extraction helper script
low_findings.description: The static analyzer correctly identified a file write operation at scripts/extract_pdf.py:53. The script opens a file for writing extracted PDF text content. This is the documented purpose of the helper script. The output path is derived from the input PDF filename, not from untrusted input. No arbitrary file write risk.

content.user_title: Assess Bias in Prognosis Studies with QUAPAS
content.value_statement: Medical researchers need to evaluate the quality of prognosis studies but lack a standardized reproducible method. This skill applies the validated QUAPAS framework to assess risk of bias across 5 domains and generate structured evaluation reports.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes prognosis study text across 5 QUAPAS domains: Participants, Index Test, Outcome, Flow and Timing, and Analysis
Determines domain-level risk of bias as Low, High, or Unclear based on standardized signaling questions
Generates structured JSON output with per-domain and overall risk of bias assessments
Extracts study identification including author name and publication year from paper text
Supports PDF text extraction through an included Python helper script for full-text analysis
Provides reproducible and documented workflow for systematic review quality assessment
limitations: Requires full paper text as input; cannot reliably analyze abstracts or summaries alone
Focuses on bias assessment only and does not perform statistical meta-analysis or data extraction
Assessment quality depends on completeness and clarity of the source paper text provided by the user
Cannot verify the accuracy of study data, results, or conclusions reported in the analyzed paper

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Screening
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review of prognosis studies needs to consistently evaluate risk of bias across dozens of papers using a standardized method.

use_cases.title: Critical Appraisal Education
use_cases.description: A medical educator teaches students how to critically appraise prognosis literature using validated and reproducible criteria.

use_cases.title: Publication Quality Check
use_cases.description: A peer reviewer or journal editor evaluates whether a submitted manuscript addresses key bias domains relevant to prognosis research.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Bias Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess the risk of bias in this prognosis study using the QUAPAS framework. Analyze each of the 5 domains and provide a structured output with per-domain and overall ratings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick quality check of a single medical paper

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Domain Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: For each of the 5 QUAPAS domains, evaluate the signaling questions and provide specific evidence from the paper text to support each answer before assigning a risk level.
prompt_templates.scenario: In-depth analysis with evidence tracking for each domain

prompt_templates.title: PDF-Based Full Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract text from the PDF file at the provided path using the extract_pdf.py script, then assess the prognosis study using the QUAPAS criteria across all 5 domains.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full-text analysis starting from a PDF document

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Multi-Study Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the risk of bias for each of the provided prognosis studies using the QUAPAS framework. Generate a comparative table showing domain-level and overall bias for all studies side by side.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comparing multiple studies for a systematic review or meta-analysis

output_examples.input: Assess bias in a prognosis study by Wang, 2018 about heart failure prediction models.
output_examples.output:
Study: Wang, 2018
D1 (Participants): Low - consecutive sample used
D2 (Index Test): Low - validated biomarker
D3 (Outcome): Unclear - outcome definition vague
D4 (Flow and Timing): Low - minimal loss to follow-up
D5 (Analysis): High - censoring not addressed
Overall: High

output_examples.input: Evaluate the methodology of this prognosis paper for a systematic review on cancer survival prediction.
output_examples.output: The analysis found Low concern for Participants domain due to consecutive enrollment and Low for Index Test due to validated assay methods. The Outcome domain showed Unclear concern because survival definitions were ambiguous. The Analysis domain raised High concern because competing events were not accounted for in the statistical methods.

output_examples.input: Compare the quality of three prognosis studies on post-surgery infection risk.
output_examples.output: Study comparison complete: Study A (Kim, 2020) showed Low bias in 4 of 5 domains with strong participant sampling. Study B (Chen, 2021) showed High bias in Analysis domain due to incomplete follow-up data. Study C (Patel, 2019) was Unclear in 3 domains due to insufficient methodological reporting. Detailed per-domain breakdown follows.

best_practices: Provide the full paper text to ensure all 5 QUAPAS domains can be assessed completely and accurately.
Document specific supporting evidence from the paper text for each signaling question answer before assigning a risk level.
Record any assumptions or judgments made when information is missing or unclear in the source paper.
anti_patterns: Running bias assessment on abstracts or summaries instead of the full paper text, which leads to incomplete and unreliable results.
Assigning domain ratings without citing specific supporting evidence from the paper text, reducing reproducibility.
Modifying the QUAPAS criteria or signaling questions without documenting the changes in the evaluation output.

faq.question: What is QUAPAS?
faq.answer: QUAPAS stands for Quality of Prognosis Studies. It is a validated tool for assessing risk of bias in prognosis research.

faq.question: How many domains does QUAPAS evaluate?
faq.answer: QUAPAS evaluates 5 domains: Participants, Index Test, Outcome, Flow and Timing, and Analysis.

faq.question: What output format does this skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill generates a JSON structure with per-domain and overall risk of bias ratings of Low, High, or Unclear.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze PDF files directly?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes a Python script called extract_pdf.py to extract text from PDF files before analysis.

faq.question: Does this skill replace human expert review?
faq.answer: No, this skill standardizes the assessment process but cannot replace expert judgment in systematic reviews.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for diagnostic accuracy studies?
faq.answer: No, QUAPAS is specific to prognosis studies. Use the QUADAS tool for diagnostic accuracy assessment instead.
